Automates locking of inactive closed issues and pull requests after two weeks of inactivity.

# Purpose
The configuration file defines a scheduled task to automatically lock closed issues and pull requests that have been inactive for two weeks. The task is triggered daily at midnight, as specified by the cron schedule. It requires write permissions for issues and pull requests to perform the locking action. The task runs on the latest Ubuntu environment and uses the `dessant/lock-threads` GitHub Action to execute the locking process. The parameters `issue-inactive-days` and `pr-inactive-days` are set to 14, indicating the period of inactivity before locking occurs.
